segments.text: Lesson 38.

translation.text: 第38课。

segments.text: Water and the Traveller.

translation.text: 水与旅行者。

segments.text: First listen, and then answer the following question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What does this text describe?

translation.text: 本文讲述什么？

segments.text: Contamination of water supplies is usually due to poor sanitation close to water sources, sewage disposal into the sources themselves, leakage of sewage into distribution systems, or contamination with

translation.text: 水源污染通常源于水源附近卫生条件差、污水直接排入水源、污水渗漏进供水系统，或受到

segments.text: industrial or farm waste.

translation.text: 工业和农业废物污染。

segments.text: Even if a piped water supply is safe at its source, it is not always safe by the time it reaches the tap.

translation.text: 即使管道供水在源头是安全的，到达水龙头时也未必安全。

segments.text: Intermittent tap water supplies should be regarded as particularly suspect.

translation.text: 间歇性自来水供应尤其应当受到怀疑。

segments.text: Travellers on short trips to areas with water supplies of uncertain quality should avoid drinking tap water or untreated water from any other source.

translation.text: 短期前往供水质量不确定地区的旅行者，应避免饮用自来水或其他来源未经处理的水。

segments.text: It is best to keep to hot drinks, bottled or canned drinks of well-known brand names.

translation.text: 最好只喝热饮，或知名品牌的瓶装、罐装饮料。

segments.text: International standards of water treatment are usually followed at bottling plants.

translation.text: 瓶装厂通常遵循国际水处理标准。

segments.text: Carbonated drinks are acidic and slightly safer.

translation.text: 碳酸饮料呈酸性，稍微安全一些。

segments.text: Make sure that all bottles are opened in your presence and that their rims are clean and dry.

translation.text: 确保所有瓶子都在你面前打开，并且瓶口清洁干燥。

segments.text: Boiling is always a good way of treating water.

translation.text: 煮沸始终是处理饮用水的好办法。

segments.text: Some hotels supplied boiled water on request, and this can be used for drinking or for brushing teeth.

translation.text: 有些旅馆可应要求提供开水，可用于饮用或刷牙。

segments.text: boiling elements that can boil small quantities of water are useful when the right voltage of electricity is available.

translation.text: 在电压合适的地方，能煮少量水的便携电热器很有用。

segments.text: Refuse politely any cold drink from an unknown source.

translation.text: 对来源不明的冷饮要礼貌拒绝。

segments.text: Ice is only as safe as the water from which it is made, and should not be put in drinks unless it is known to be safe.

translation.text: 冰块的安全程度取决于制冰用水；除非知道它安全，否则不应放进饮料。

segments.text: Drinks can be cooled by placing them on ice rather than adding ice to them.

translation.text: 可以把饮料放在冰上冷却，而不要把冰加入饮料。

segments.text: may be a medical disinfectant, but should not be relied upon to sterilize water.

translation.text: 酒精也许是医用消毒剂，却不应依靠它给水灭菌。

segments.text: Ethanol is more effective at a concentration of 50 to 70 percent.

translation.text: 浓度为50%至70%的乙醇更有效。

segments.text: Below 20 percent, its bactericidal action is negligible.

translation.text: 低于20%时，它的杀菌作用微乎其微。

segments.text: Spirits labeled 95 proof contain only about 47 percent alcohol.

translation.text: 标为95 proof酒度的烈酒只含约47%的酒精。

segments.text: Beware of methylated alcohol, which is very poisonous and should never be added to drinking water.

translation.text: 要当心工业酒精，它毒性很强，绝不能加入饮用水。

segments.text: If no other safe water supply can be obtained, tap water that is too hot to touch can be left to cool and is generally safe to drink.

translation.text: 如果无法获得其他安全水源，可让烫得无法触摸的自来水冷却，通常即可安全饮用。

segments.text: Those planning a trip to remote areas or intending to live in countries where drinking water is not readily available should know about the various possible methods for making water safe.

translation.text: 计划去偏远地区旅行，或打算在不易获得饮用水的国家生活的人，应了解使水安全的各种方法。
